source.title: Negative valence systems: sustained threat and the predatory imminence continuum

confidence_rationale: Strong mapping fidelity: predatory imminence continuum theory is the ethological formalisation of ARC-024's gradient structure claim, providing both the theoretical grounding and neural circuit detail. Pre-encounter, post-encounter, and circa-strike modes directly instantiate the gradient: behaviour form and vigour both change continuously as a function of perceived threat distance, before contact occurs. Source quality is solid (Fanselow is the originator of PIC theory, open-access PMC journal). Transfer risk is moderate (primarily animal literature; human data exists but is secondary).
failure_signatures: PIC theory describes three discrete modes rather than a fully continuous gradient; the transitions between modes are threshold-like, which could be interpreted as arguing for partial discreteness rather than pure continuous gradient structure.
Primarily rodent literature; human PIC research (including Mobbs 2007) is referenced but not the primary evidence base of this review.
The review's focus is on sustained threat and BST/hippocampal circuits rather than the gradient structure per se; the gradient argument must be inferred from the broader PIC framework.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The predatory imminence continuum ranges from no threat (pre-encounter: anxiety, foraging modification) through detected threat (post-encounter: freezing, inhibition) to near-contact (circa-strike: fight/flight/tonic immobility). Behaviour topography and vigour are determined by the psychological distance from the threat, not a binary contact signal.
mapping.ree_translation: The predatory imminence continuum is the behavioural instantiation of ARC-024's asymptotic gradient: harm signal magnitude and response topography grade continuously with proximity to the harm source. The hazard_field in CausalGridWorldV2 (continuous proximity signal) recapitulates this gradient; E3.harm_eval learning graded danger maps (EXQ-029 PASS) mirrors the PIC's topographic differentiation across gradient levels.
mapping.mapping_caveat: PIC is a behavioural/neural theory of anti-predator defence; ARC-024 is an architectural claim about world-latent space representation. The inference from observable behavioural gradient to world-latent gradient representation requires treating the world's harm signal as the causal driver of the PIC gradient, which is the core architectural assumption ARC-024 makes.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review integrating rodent fear conditioning literature and human data; framed around NIMH RDoC negative valence constructs

confidence_components.notes: Highest mapping fidelity in this batch: PIC directly models graded harm signals before contact. Source quality penalised slightly for journal tier (Emerging Topics Life Sciences vs Science/Nature) and single-author review format. Transfer risk low because the animal-to-human transfer is supported by Mobbs 2007 and the PIC literature includes human data.
